channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft starts testing an AI-based search engine in Windows 11
item.description: Windows Insiders can now download a new beta version of Windows 11 that offers support for Microsoft’s new search engine based on artificial intelligence (AI); that means, among other things, it is possible to use natural language when performing a search.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One of the worst Resident Evil games could be getting another re-release according to new ESRB rating
item.description: Resident Evil 6, specifically, could be getting a current-generation release according to a new ESRB rating.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Analyst says there are those in the gaming industry who ‘hope’ GTA 6 will cost up to $100
item.description: A gaming analyst has claimed that there are those in the games industry who are hoping GTA 6 will cost up to $100.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Las empresas son más conscientes de la huella medioambiental de la IA generativa, pero tienen problemas para abordarla
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa tiene un creciente y significativo impacto negativo en el medioambiente. A pesar de ello, muchas organizaciones no están haciendo un seguimiento adecuado de la huella que generan, lo que está poniendo en peligro sus objetivos ESG.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump charges DOGE with modernizing federal technology and software
item.description: DOGE, the whimsically named Department of Government Efficiency proposed by Elon Musk as a way to save $2 trillion in US federal government spending, is now a reality.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Don’t buy a new iPad Air – a new M3 model has been tipped to land early this year
item.description: A new handful of rumors seem to corroborate the upcoming release of an M3-powered iPad Air, the first tablet to feature the chipset after the iPad Pro skipped straight to the M4.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Netflix caught up in AI Oscars controversy alongside The Brutalist as Emilia Pérez criticized for using voice enhancement tech
item.description: The Brutalist director, Brady Corbet, weighs in on the AI Oscars controversy as Netflix's Emilia Pérez also comes under scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 backwards compatibility is ‘the best direction to take’ for players, Nintendo says
item.description: Backwards compatibility on the Nintendo Switch 2 was the “best decision” for all consumers, according to Nintendo.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US GPU export limits could bring cold war to AI, data center markets
item.description: The US Biden administration, during its final week in office, issued a rule limiting sales of powerful AI chips to more than 100 countries, to keep the technology out of the hands of Chinese companies and the military there.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump orders government not to infringe on Americans’ speech, calls for censorship investigation
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered that no federal officer, employee or agent may unconstitutionally abridge the free speech of any American citizen.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vivek Ramaswamy set to depart DOGE
item.description: Biotech entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy confirmed on Monday he will not serve on President Trump’s “Department of Government Efficiency” (DOGE) commission, leaving Tesla CEO Elon Musk to singularly lead the advisory group.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: 5 hot network trends for 2025
item.description: Networking has been a relatively stable industry for decades. On the vendor side of the equation, it’s been Cisco versus everyone else. On the tech side, a slow, steady progression of incrementally faster versions of tried-and-true standards like Wi-Fi and Ethernet was the norm.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also are among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Build your focus stack with these proven tools and gadgets
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. A dozen tabs open. Notifications pinging. I just opened my laptop and unread emails are already calling my name.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Trump's inauguration will usher in a crypto-friendly administration, and with it, new state policies
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The bitcoin-friendly administration of President-elect Donald Trump and an expanding lobbying effort in statehouses could push states to become more open to crypto and lead public pension funds and treasuries to buy into it.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How to stop robocalls on your flip phone
item.description: Flip phones are harder to block spam calls from, but it's not impossible. Kurt the "CyberGuy" explains how to stop incoming robocalls on your flip phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Secure AI? Dream on, says AI red team
item.description: The group responsible for red teaming of over 100 generative AI products at Microsoft has concluded that the work of building safe and secure AI systems will never be complete.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok refugees are pouring to Xiaohongshu. Here's what you need to know about the RedNote app
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the fate of TikTok hangs in the balance, U.S. TikTok users are flocking to the Chinese social media app Xiaohongshu, also called RedNote – making it the top downloaded app in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FU TING and DAVID COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who could buy TikTok? Only a few parties are serious about offering
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The supremely popular TikTok could be banned on Jan. 19 under a federal law that forces the video sharing platform to divest itself from its China-based parent company, ByteDance, or shut down its U.S. operations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Bitcoin soars past $100,000 ahead of possible early action on crypto by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The price of bitcoin topped $100,000 again early Friday as a pumped up cryptocurrency industry expects early action by Donald Trump when he's sworn in as president next week.
item.dc:creator: ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Chinese tech firm shares robot training secrets with the world
item.description: A pioneering artificial intelligence and robotics company from China has developed a repository of over 1 million robotic movements to provide robotic training.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's new executive order aims to shore up US cyber defenses
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden issued an executive order Thursday aimed at strengthening the nation's cybersecurity and making it easier to go after foreign adversaries or hacking groups that try to compromise U.S. internet and telecommunication systems.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nile dials-up AI to simplify network provisioning, operation
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added an AI-based tool aimed at helping enterprise customers provision and operate the vendor’s Campus Network-as-a-Service deployments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google ups Workspace price, makes Gemini AI features available for free
item.description: Google will no longer charge Workspace customers an additional fee for Gemini, expanding access to generative AI (genAI) features in apps such as Gmail, Docs, and Sheets. At the same time, the company is increasing the price of Workspace subscriptions by a small amount.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Digital Transformation in Prisons: How Kazakhstan is Leading the Way
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kazakhstan’s AI-Powered Prisons: Setting a New Standard for Safety and Efficiency
item.description: Technology innovations have made the digital transformation of public services essential to enhancing safety, efficiency, and accountability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Red Hat offers OpenShift Virtualization Engine, Kubernetes Connectivity Link
item.description: Red Hat has made both its Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization Engine and Red Hat Connectivity Link software generally available. The company also announced Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management for Virtualization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg will cohost reception with Republican billionaires for Trump inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is cohosting a reception with billionaire Republican donors next week for Donald Trump’s inauguration, the latest sign of the Facebook founder's embrace of the president-elect.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Court lets Tennessee porn site age verification law take effect as Texas law goes to Supreme Court
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — A panel of appeals judges has ruled that Tennessee can begin enforcing a law that requires pornographic websites to verify their visitors’ age as the First Amendment debate rises to the U.S. Supreme Court this week for oral arguments over a similar Texas law.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Technology executives on the move at Education, OPM, HHS
item.description: Steve Hernandez, Education’s CISO, and Brian Bordelon, Education’s deputy CIO, have moved to new agencies to take on similar roles. The post Technology executives on the move at Education, OPM, HHS first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
